---
title: "NullPay walkthrough"
subtitle: "A real privacy-payments dApp, dissected end-to-end."
---

::: {.callout-note}
**Status:** Stub. Full chapter coming soon.
:::

## The question

You've now read about all the primitives — records, mappings, commitments,
view keys, Merkle proofs, the credit register, USDCx compliance. How do
they compose into an actual product?

[NullPay](https://github.com/casualcomputer/aleo-notebooks) is a working
anonymous-invoice dApp on Aleo testnet/mainnet. Two Leo programs, ~14
transitions, multi-currency support (credits + stablecoins), encrypted
off-chain metadata in a Postgres-style backend, burner-wallet UX, optional
auditor disclosure. Real code, real deployment.

This chapter walks through every architectural decision in NullPay and
explains why it was made.

## What this chapter will cover

- The 14 transitions, grouped by privacy class:
  - `create_invoice_*` (one per currency)
  - `pay_invoice_*` / `pay_donation_*`
  - `settle_invoice`, `delete_invoice`, `get_invoice_status`
- The on-chain commitment: BHP256 hash of the invoice JSON, stored in
  `mapping invoices`
- The off-chain encrypted store: AES-256-GCM in Supabase, keyed by commitment
- The burner wallet: encrypted backup pattern stored on-chain
- The oracle quote pattern: prices signed off-chain, verified on-chain
- The MCP server (`@nullpay/mcp`): how NullPay exposes its API to AI agents
- The compliance integration: USDCx compliance proofs flow through NullPay

## Interactive viz planned

A full transaction flow simulator: click "create invoice → pay invoice →
settle" and watch every state change in:
- The on-chain mapping (commitments → status)
- Each consumed record (nullifiers ticking)
- The off-chain Supabase blobs (which fields appear/decrypt for which party)
- The Telegram notification fired to the merchant

## Why this is the capstone chapter

Every primitive you've read about appears here, in production code, doing
real work. If you can finish this chapter and explain *why each design
decision was made*, you can architect your own Aleo dApp.

## Source code

- Repo: [github.com/casualcomputer/aleo-experiments/tree/main/NullPay](https://github.com/casualcomputer/aleo-experiments/tree/main/NullPay)
  *(or wherever it ends up — replace before publish)*
- Live demo: [nullpay.app](https://nullpay.app) *(or wherever)*
- Architecture docs: see the in-tree `docs/` folder
